summary: The Dow rose 332 points, or 0.6%, to finish at a record high. Despite the downward slide of oil prices, trading ahead of Wednesday’s Fed interest rate decision was relatively “light and featureless,” writes Joe Mazzola, head trading and derivatives strategist at Charles Schwab. Kevin Warsh’s first press conference as Fed chairman is the big event tomorrow (more on that below), but the U.S. Census is also set to release a significant economic barometer on Wednesday morning: May retail sales.
content: The Dow rose 332 points, or 0.6%, to finish at a record high. Despite the downward slide of oil prices, trading ahead of Wednesday’s Fed interest rate decision was relatively “light and featureless,” writes Joe Mazzola, head trading and derivatives strategist at Charles Schwab. Kevin Warsh’s first press conference as Fed chairman is the big event tomorrow (more on that below), but the U.S. Census is also set to release a significant economic barometer on Wednesday morning: May retail sales.
chineseSummary: 道琼斯指数收涨 332 点，即 0.6%，创历史新高。嘉信理财首席交易和衍生品策略师乔·马佐拉(Joe Mazzola)写道，尽管油价下跌，但周三美联储利率决定前的交易相对“清淡且无特色”。凯文·沃什 (Kevin Warsh) 作为美联储主席的首次新闻发布会是明天的重大事件（更多内容见下文），但美国人口普查局也将于周三上午发布重要的经济晴雨表：5 月份零售销售。

whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
